title: Populate real SHA-256 hashes in ferrotorch-hub registry (audit follow-up)
description:
Follow-up from audit #738 closure (ferrotorch-hub finding #6).

After landing the fail-fast SHA-256 policy in download::download_and_verify, every entry in registry.rs that still uses the all-zero placeholder hash will refuse to download with InvalidArgument. The placeholders were intentional during initial wiring but are now blocking. To restore download capability for each model:

1. Upload the canonical safetensors weight to https://huggingface.co/ferrotorch/<model> for each registry entry (resnet18/34/50, vgg11/16, vit_b_16, efficientnet_b0, swin_tiny, convnext_tiny, unet, yolo, mobilenet_v2, mobilenet_v3_small, densenet121, inception_v3 — 15 models).
2. Compute SHA-256 of the uploaded artefact.
3. Replace the all-zero placeholder string in the corresponding ModelInfo entry with the real 64-char hex digest.
4. Add an integration test that downloads, verifies, and matches the hash for at least one entry.

Until this is done, ferrotorch_hub::load_pretrained returns Err for the affected models — that is the deliberate policy after the audit's #6 finding (silent integrity bypass).
